items.rationale: Sees blockchains as a counterbalance to centralized AI, enabling permissionless markets for compute/data and provenance tracking against deepfakes, democratizing AI innovation.
items.age_note: Most substantive on 2023-12-06; not restated in March 2024 but recent enough and consistent with his decentralization thesis

citations.quote: Decentralized blockchains are a counterbalancing force to centralized AI.
Decentralized, open-source crypto networks will democratize (vs. concentrate) AI innovation, ultimately making it safer for consumers.

items.rationale: Believes converting passive crypto owners into active on-chain users is a bigger growth opportunity than expanding the investor count; tracks non-financial adoption metrics now at all-time highs.

citations.quote: Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users. This is more important... than expanding the total number of crypto investors.
The State of Crypto Index is at an all time high. The index measures innovation and adoption... based on 14 key non-financial metrics.

items.rationale: Views a decentralized computing platform owned by no single entity as a necessary counterbalance to extreme concentration of traffic and market cap among a handful of companies.
items.age_note: Core thesis stated 2023-04-16; reinforced by ongoing 2024 messaging on builder permissionlessness and platform commitments

citations.quote: A decentralized computing platform, owned by no single entity, is a counterbalancing force to this increasing consolidation of power. The internet needs web3.

items.rationale: Excited by new SNARK paradigms (Lasso, Jolt) as moving the industry forward; repeatedly highlights ZK verification as foundational infrastructure progress.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-08-10 / 2023-09-08; not restated since but never reversed

citations.quote: Lasso and Jolt represent a new paradigm in SNARK design.

items.rationale: Frames staking as securing a multi-hundred-billion-dollar economy rather than mere yield, and praises decentralization efforts in staking infrastructure.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-02 to 2023-05; older view, not restated near 2024-03-31

citations.quote: Staking is not just about earning yield. On Ethereum, it's used to secure a $400 billion economy of ETH, ERC20 tokens, and NFTs.

items.rationale: Sees progress toward resilient, decentralized social as a major milestone and part of platforms making credible commitments to builders.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-10-11; not restated near analysis date

citations.quote: A huge milestone crossed on the path toward more resilient, decentralized social networks for the world.

items.rationale: Welcomes expansion of legal pathways for DAOs to operate, signaling enthusiasm for maturing organizational/legal infrastructure.

citations.quote: Huge news for expanding the ways in which DAOs can legally operate.

items.rationale: Worried the US is losing its web3 lead and that critical infrastructure development will move offshore to untrusted suppliers; frames keeping crypto onshore as national security.

citations.quote: Keeping crypto onshore is a matter of national security... We should not have to rely on untrusted foreign developers and suppliers for critical technology infrastructure.
there are signs that the US may be losing its lead in web3.

items.rationale: Concerned that AI models can only be trained/operated by a handful of tech giants due to compute and data costs, and that one actor could end up deciding for all.

citations.quote: AI models... can currently only be trained and operated by a handful of tech giants, since the required compute and training data are prohibitive for smaller players.

items.rationale: Pushes back on speculation being conflated with crypto's value proposition, implicitly concerned that market narratives over-index on price rather than fundamentals.

citations.quote: In the crypto market, speculation is a byproduct that is separate from its core value proposition.

items.rationale: Notes developers fear building on big web2 platforms that can shut them down at any time, contrasting with blockchains' credible commitments.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-04-14; older but consistent with current thesis

citations.quote: Developers today are scared to build on top of the big web2 platforms - who can shut them down at any time.

items.rationale: Treats ETH as the asset securing a $400B on-chain economy via staking; central to his Ethereum infrastructure and adoption thesis, though framed on fundamentals not price.
items.age_note: Primarily 2023 statements; positive framing not restated near 2024-03-31 and never about price targets

citations.quote: On Ethereum, it's used to secure a $400 billion economy of ETH, ERC20 tokens, and NFTs.

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish on crypto adoption — points to an all-time-high non-financial adoption index, growing on-chain usage, and maturing infrastructure (ZK, staking, DAOs, AI). But his bullishness is explicitly fundamentals-driven, not speculative: he separates speculation from value and frames the main risk as US regulatory/offshoring policy rather than market downside. So constructive long-term posture, deliberately price-agnostic.

notes: Matsuoka is a data/research voice at a16z crypto, so his statements emphasize adoption metrics, infrastructure, and policy rather than tradeable token calls or near-term price views — virtually no tactical-horizon positioning exists in the corpus. Most-recent (March 2024) views: closing the owner-to-active-user gap, all-time-high adoption index, separating speculation from value, US-onshoring/national-security policy concern, and DAO legal expansion. The AI+blockchain thesis (Dec 2023) is recent and substantive but not restated in March. ZK/SNARK, staking, and decentralized social enthusiasm are persisted from 2023 and not restated near the analysis date (cited with age notes). No reversals detected — his decentralization-as-counterbalance thesis is consistent throughout. Testnet ETH (Goerli/Sepolia) and Lido/LDO references are operational/community, not investment views, so excluded from tokens.
